description: A false-colour satellite image depicts a coastal landmass, specifically showing an area identified as "Forest damaged by fire in Benicolet (Spain)". The image was captured by SPOT-5 (2.5 m) using a 1/2/3 Band Combination on 2011-06-22. The source is CNES 2011©, distributed by Airbus DS/Spot Image. The dark blue area represents the sea. The landmass primarily features reddish-pink areas, indicating healthy dense vegetation or forest. Light yellow patches are scattered across the land, likely representing other land cover types such as agricultural fields or sparsely vegetated areas. A distinct central area of the landmass is covered in greenish tones, suggesting a different type of healthy forest or dense vegetation. An irregular, compact dark red patch is visible within the reddish-pink forested area, clearly delineating the forest area affected by fire.
